oai_dc:dc.dc:title: Introducing the Gold Bourke's Parakeet
oai_dc:dc.dc:creator: Maldonado, Nelson
oai_dc:dc.dc:description: The Bourke's parakeet (Neopsephotus Bourkii), also known as Bourke's parrot, is a small and colorful parrot native to Australia. It measures about 7-8 inches long, with a soft pinkish underbelly, blue wings, and a brownish-gray back. Males often have brighter blue on their foreheads compared to females. They are known for their gentle and quiet demeanor, making them popular as pets. Today, I am proud and excited to introduce five new Gold Bourke parakeet (Neopsephotus Bourkii) mutations. The discovery and development of this gold line began in 2016 in our aviary (Los Monos Aviary, LLC.) located in Chambersburg, Pennsylvania. [For the full article, with images, please open the PDF link below]
oai_dc:dc.dc:source: AFA Watchbird; Vol. 52 No. 1 (2025): Volume 52 July 2025 issue of AFA Watchbird
